summary: Completed comprehensive testing of redesigned single-view table-based POS interface. All backend APIs (26/26 tests passed) and frontend UI features working correctly. Main POS view, table grid, order panel, kitchen orders, bill generation, and navigation all functional.

issues: Test data from backend tests creates many TEST-prefixed tables that clutter the view - consider cleanup script or filtering

passed_tests: Main POS view loads with table grid showing all tables
Available tables show grey (#E8E5E0)
Occupied tables show yellow (#FDE68A)
Ready tables show green (#BBF7D0)
Clicking a table opens right panel with menu items and categories
Category filtering works (All, Main Course, Salad, Dessert, Beverage, Appetizer)
Menu search functionality works
Add items to cart from right panel using + button
Adjust quantities with +/- buttons
Remove items with X button
Order total calculates correctly
Place order for a table - table turns yellow (occupied)
Kitchen orders section shows pending orders with 'Start Preparing' button
Start Preparing changes order status to 'preparing'
Mark Ready changes order status to 'ready' and table turns green
Print icon on occupied/ready table opens bill dialog
Bill dialog shows items, subtotal, tax (18%), and total
Payment method selection (Cash, Card, UPI) works
Generate Bill marks order completed and table back to available (grey)
Top bar navigation to Kitchen page works
Top bar navigation to Admin page works
Top bar navigation to Owner page works
Owner panel password protected at /owner
Owner login with 'owner123' grants access to Staff Management
Refresh button reloads data
Close panel button (X) closes the order panel
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created TEST-prefixed tables, menu items, staff, and orders for testing purposes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Complete redesign from multi-page to single-page POS tested successfully. MainPOS.jsx is the main entry point. Kitchen, Admin, Owner pages accessible via top navigation. All CRUD operations for tables, menu, orders, bills, staff, attendance, and salary working. Test data cleanup may be needed before production.
